narrative:
Elliot Anderson is a central midfielder for the United Kingdom. His overall rating of 7.15/10 places him in the top 4% of central midfields at this tournament. This rating is significantly influenced by his club form over the last two seasons, accounting for 67% of the total.

Anderson demonstrates strong defensive contributions at this World Cup, making 3.051 tackles per 90 minutes and 1.525 interceptions per 90 minutes. He also contributes proactively with 0.218 assists per 90 minutes over 413 minutes played. However, his attacking output shows room for growth, with an expected goals (xG) of 0.05 per 90 minutes and 0.872 shots per 90 minutes at this tournament.

Anderson appears to be a key presence in central midfield for his national team, linking defensive stability with creative moments. Scouts and fans should observe his continued defensive work rate and watch for any increase in his attacking metrics, particularly his shots and xG per 90 minutes. His influence on both sides of the ball will be crucial for his team's progression.
